source_compatibility: Factory Agent Readiness 82-signal baseline, independently owned and adapted

scoring.owned_applicability: exclude not_applicable applications from each criterion denominator
scoring.compatibility_applicability: count mixed not_applicable applications in the app denominator; skip only when all are not_applicable

criteria.title: Large File Detection
criteria.guidance: Automated tooling prevents or flags oversized source/binary files through hooks, CI, LFS policy, or enforced file-size rules.

criteria.title: Tech Debt Tracking
criteria.guidance: CI, linters, or a documented tracked system detects TODO/FIXME or otherwise maintains actionable technical-debt records.

criteria.title: Build Command Documented
criteria.guidance: Root agent/developer documentation gives a working build command or equivalent installation/build sequence.

criteria.title: Dependencies Pinned
criteria.guidance: A committed lockfile or exact dependency resolution mechanism makes installs reproducible.

criteria.title: VCS CLI Tools
criteria.guidance: The repository's VCS CLI is installed and authenticated, enabling programmatic PR, issue, workflow, and policy inspection.

criteria.title: Automated PR Review
criteria.guidance: Automation generates substantive review comments or assessments on pull requests, not only pass/fail checks.

criteria.title: Agentic Development
criteria.guidance: History, workflows, scripts, skills, or configuration demonstrate that coding agents actively participate in development.

criteria.title: Fast CI Feedback
criteria.guidance: Measured primary CI feedback for representative pull requests completes in under ten minutes.

criteria.title: Build Performance Tracking
criteria.guidance: Build duration or size is deliberately measured, trended, cached, budgeted, or optimized with observable evidence.

criteria.title: Deployment Frequency
criteria.guidance: Deployment records show automated successful releases multiple times per week when active development warrants it.

criteria.title: Single Command Setup
criteria.guidance: A documented single command or short copy-paste sequence takes a fresh clone to a runnable development environment.

criteria.title: Feature Flag Infrastructure
criteria.guidance: A real platform or custom registry/evaluation system supports controlled feature activation.

criteria.title: Release Notes Automation
criteria.guidance: Release notes or changelogs are generated automatically on releases or a documented recurring cadence.

criteria.title: Progressive Rollout
criteria.guidance: Deployment infrastructure supports canary, percentage, ring, or equivalent limited-audience rollout.

criteria.title: Rollback Automation
criteria.guidance: A documented, tested one-click or automated rollback restores the deployed system without deep manual investigation.

criteria.title: Monorepo Tooling
criteria.guidance: Multi-package repositories define workspaces/modules and enforce or orchestrate component boundaries.

criteria.title: Version Drift Detection
criteria.guidance: Automation detects or prevents inconsistent dependency versions across monorepo packages.

criteria.title: Release Automation
criteria.guidance: A CD, GitOps, image publishing, or release workflow automates shipping rather than relying on undocumented manual commands.

criteria.title: Dead Feature Flag Detection
criteria.guidance: Given feature flags exist, automation or a lifecycle workflow detects unused, stale, or overdue flags and drives cleanup.

criteria.title: AGENTS.md
criteria.guidance: A useful non-empty root AGENTS.md documents commands, workflow, and project conventions for autonomous agents.

criteria.title: README
criteria.guidance: A root README provides accurate setup, purpose, and usage instructions.

criteria.title: Automated Documentation Generation
criteria.guidance: Runnable tooling creates or updates useful documentation artifacts and demonstrates more than static prose.

criteria.title: Agent Skills
criteria.guidance: At least one valid, useful SKILL.md package exists in a discoverable skills directory.

criteria.title: Documentation Freshness
criteria.guidance: Key agent/developer documentation has been updated within the last 180 days.

criteria.title: Service Flow Documented
criteria.guidance: Architecture diagrams or service-dependency documentation explain important runtime flows and external systems.

criteria.title: AGENTS.md Validation
criteria.guidance: Automation checks AGENTS.md accuracy through command execution, link/reference checks, generation, or meaningful drift validation—not length alone.

criteria.title: Devcontainer
criteria.guidance: A devcontainer configuration captures the language/toolchain and development prerequisites.

criteria.title: Environment Template
criteria.guidance: Environment templates or exact documentation enumerate required runtime variables without committing secrets.

criteria.title: Local Services Setup
criteria.guidance: External development dependencies have executable local setup such as Compose, testcontainers, or precise service instructions.

criteria.title: Devcontainer Runnable
criteria.guidance: The devcontainer has been built and started successfully with available tooling, not merely committed.

criteria.title: Runbooks Documented
criteria.guidance: Repository docs point operators and agents to actionable incident/runbook procedures.

criteria.title: Branch Protection
criteria.guidance: Verified repository rules protect the primary branch from unsafe direct changes, force pushes, or deletion.

criteria.title: Secret Scanning
criteria.guidance: Native or repository automation detects committed/staged secrets and is verifiably enabled.

criteria.title: CODEOWNERS
criteria.guidance: A valid CODEOWNERS file assigns meaningful ownership to repository paths.

criteria.title: Automated Security Review
criteria.guidance: Automation generates readable SAST, dependency, container, or security assessment results—not only a status light.

criteria.title: Dependency Update Automation
criteria.guidance: Dependabot, Renovate, or equivalent automation actively proposes dependency updates.

criteria.title: Comprehensive Gitignore
criteria.guidance: Ignore rules cover secrets, dependencies, builds, caches, editor metadata, and OS artifacts appropriate to the repository.

criteria.title: Privacy Compliance
criteria.guidance: Systems processing personal data implement or document applicable consent, retention, access/deletion, minimization, or anonymization controls.

criteria.title: Secrets Management
criteria.guidance: Secrets use a secure manager or documented environment/CI secret pattern; no hardcoded-secret workflow is required.

criteria.title: Minimum Release Age
criteria.guidance: An explicit policy or automated gate delays adoption of newly published dependency versions for a defined period.

criteria.title: Issue Templates
criteria.guidance: Structured issue templates collect useful bug, feature, or task context.

criteria.title: Issue Labeling System
criteria.guidance: Consistent priority, type, and area labels enable programmatic issue filtering and prioritization.

criteria.title: Backlog Health
criteria.guidance: A representative open-issue sample is descriptive, labeled, and not predominantly abandoned for over a year.

criteria.title: Pull Request Templates
criteria.guidance: Pull/merge request templates require description, validation, and relevant operational context.

criteria.title: Lint Configuration
criteria.guidance: The application has an effective linter or equivalent static quality analyzer.

criteria.title: Type Checking
criteria.guidance: The application has an executable type checker or a language compiler providing equivalent checks.

criteria.title: Formatter
criteria.guidance: A configured formatter covers the application's source.

criteria.title: Pre-commit Hooks
criteria.guidance: Commit-time hooks run relevant validation for the application without unsafe whole-tree behavior.

criteria.title: Strict Typing
criteria.guidance: Strict type checking is enabled or inherent to the language and enforced for application source.

criteria.title: Naming Consistency
criteria.guidance: Lint rules, compiler conventions, or explicit enforced documentation maintain consistent names.

criteria.title: Cyclomatic Complexity
criteria.guidance: Complexity is analyzed with enforceable thresholds or a monitored quality gate.

criteria.title: Dead Code Detection
criteria.guidance: Tooling detects unused exports, modules, symbols, or unreachable application code.

criteria.title: Duplicate Code Detection
criteria.guidance: Automated duplication analysis covers the application's code and enforces or reports a meaningful threshold.

criteria.title: Code Modularization
criteria.guidance: Meaningful architecture/module boundaries are compiler- or tool-enforced for the application.

criteria.title: N+1 Detection
criteria.guidance: For the application's repeated data-access or request fan-out surface, tooling detects N+1 behavior through runtime analysis, tests, query tracking, DataLoader, or APM.

criteria.title: Heavy Dependency Detection
criteria.guidance: For the application's deploy/runtime artifact, tooling attributes heavy dependencies or bundle/install impact and reports or budgets changes.

criteria.title: Unused Dependencies Detection
criteria.guidance: Tooling identifies declared but unused dependencies for the application.

criteria.title: Unit Tests Exist
criteria.guidance: Behavior-focused unit tests exist for application logic.

criteria.title: Integration Tests Exist
criteria.guidance: Tests exercise meaningful integration boundaries, browser flows, services, or persistence for the application.

criteria.title: Unit Tests Runnable
criteria.guidance: The documented local test command can discover or execute application tests in the current environment.

criteria.title: Test Performance Tracking
criteria.guidance: Test duration is deliberately measured, reported, trended, or budgeted.

criteria.title: Flaky Test Detection
criteria.guidance: Retries, repeated probes, quarantine policy, or test analytics proactively detect and manage flaky tests.

criteria.title: Test Coverage Thresholds
criteria.guidance: Minimum application coverage is enforced by the test runner, CI, or a blocking quality gate.

criteria.title: Test Naming Conventions
criteria.guidance: Framework configuration, language convention, or repository policy defines discoverable test file naming.

criteria.title: Test Isolation
criteria.guidance: Parallelism, isolated databases, transactions, testcontainers, factories, or randomization prevent cross-test coupling.

criteria.title: API Schema Documentation
criteria.guidance: Applications exposing service APIs provide a first-party OpenAPI/Swagger or GraphQL schema that is valid and maintained.

criteria.title: Database Schema
criteria.guidance: Applications with persistence expose schema definitions or migrations sufficient for an agent to understand and safely change the data model.

criteria.title: Structured Logging
criteria.guidance: A structured logging library or dedicated logger emits machine-queryable application logs.

criteria.title: Distributed Tracing
criteria.guidance: Trace/request identifiers or OpenTelemetry context propagate through relevant request and service boundaries.

criteria.title: Metrics Collection
criteria.guidance: Operational telemetry records meaningful application performance or behavior metrics.

criteria.title: Code Quality Metrics
criteria.guidance: Coverage, complexity, duplication, maintainability, or comparable code-quality measurements are monitored for the application.

criteria.title: Contextualized Error Tracking
criteria.guidance: Error tracking includes actionable stack/source mapping plus relevant breadcrumbs, tags, request, release, or safe user context.

criteria.title: Alerting Configured
criteria.guidance: Actionable alert rules notify operators when important application failures or thresholds occur.

criteria.title: Deployment Observability
criteria.guidance: Agents/operators can correlate deployments with health through documented dashboards, logs, annotations, or notifications.

criteria.title: Health Checks
criteria.guidance: Deployed application surfaces have working health/readiness mechanisms appropriate to their runtime.

criteria.title: Circuit Breakers
criteria.guidance: Applications calling external systems implement circuit breaking, bounded retries/backoff, or a tested resilience fallback.

criteria.title: Profiling Instrumentation
criteria.guidance: Production or development profiling can capture CPU, memory, flamegraph, APM, or continuous-profile evidence for meaningful performance work.

criteria.title: DAST Scanning
criteria.guidance: CI runs dynamic security testing against a running web application or equivalent deployed test surface.

criteria.title: PII Handling
criteria.guidance: Applications processing personal data have tooling or concrete documented procedures for classification, masking, minimization, and safe handling.

criteria.title: Log Scrubbing
criteria.guidance: Configured redaction/sanitization prevents secrets and sensitive personal data from entering logs and error telemetry.

criteria.title: Product Analytics
criteria.guidance: User-facing product behavior is instrumented in an analytics system so feature adoption and impact can be measured.

criteria.title: Error-to-Insight Pipeline
criteria.guidance: Error tracking connects to issue/incident creation or an equivalent automated workflow that converts failures into actionable tracked work.
